ai_strategy_lead.name: CIO Responsible AI / UCSB AI major
ai_strategy_lead.title: Public AI strategy or initiative lead / hub identified from cited sources

ai_strategy.summary: University of California, Santa Barbara has a public AI posture anchored in CIO Responsible AI / UCSB AI major. The cited sources show activity across responsible-use guidance, teaching resources, research or infrastructure, and AI literacy or workforce preparation.

key_provisions: Use AI transparently and follow course, research, workplace and academic-integrity expectations.
Do not enter confidential, regulated, sensitive, unpublished or protected institutional data into unapproved public AI tools.
Human users remain accountable for accuracy, bias, copyright, privacy and final decisions based on AI output.
ai_policy.summary: University of California, Santa Barbara publishes AI policy or guidance with a comprehensive stance: AI use is allowed or actively explored, but bounded by privacy, ethics, academic integrity, compliance and human-review expectations.

bodies.name: CIO Responsible AI / UCSB AI major
bodies.scope: Public AI strategy, guidance, teaching, research or technology coordination.

governance_structure.summary: AI governance at University of California, Santa Barbara is evidenced through CIO Responsible AI / UCSB AI major and the cited policy or strategy sources. Public sources did not identify a separate chief AI officer during this sweep.

initiatives.title: Responsible AI policy and guidance

initiatives.significance: This is a concrete public signal of how the institution is translating AI into policy, teaching, research, operations, workforce preparation, or access.

investment.description: No public dollar amount disclosed for this initiative.

collaborations.description: No external collaboration identified in the cited source.

initiatives.policy_implications: Sets institutional guardrails for classroom, research, staff and administrative use.

initiatives.title: AI and Teaching and Learning assessment guidance
initiatives.description: UCSB's Office of Teaching and Learning maintains faculty guidance for generative AI that includes redesigning assignments, clarifying acceptable AI use, and using AI-aware assessment practices instead of relying only on detection.
initiatives.significance: The guidance makes assessment redesign a visible part of UCSB's AI teaching posture, closing a gap beyond policy-only language.

investment.description: Public guidance does not disclose a separate assessment-redesign budget.

collaborations.description: The cited source is an internal faculty-development resource.

initiatives.policy_implications: Faculty are directed toward explicit AI-use expectations and assessment design choices.

computing_infrastructure.summary: UCSB's ACTION AI Institute uses a public advanced-computing allocation rather than a disclosed campus-owned GPU system; the institute source reports access to 750,000 GPU and CPU core hours through San Diego Supercomputer Center.

investments_and_gifts.title: NSF AI Institute for Agent-based Cyber Threat Intelligence and Operation
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Seven-university AI institute led by UCSB to develop agent-based cyber threat intelligence and response technologies.

regulatory_context.summary: University of California, Santa Barbara operates within a state or system AI-policy context cited in this profile.

narrative_summary: University of California, Santa Barbara has a documented AI posture across policy, teaching, research, infrastructure or access. The central public signal is CIO Responsible AI / UCSB AI major, supported by policy or guidance sources that encourage responsible use while emphasizing privacy, academic integrity, ethics and human oversight. The institution also shows curriculum or AI literacy activity and at least one research, center, infrastructure or applied innovation signal. Its maturity is strongest where public sources show durable structures: committees, centers, portals, degree programs, supercomputing, enterprise tools, or workforce initiatives. The gaps are also important: this sweep did not find a standalone chief AI officer in public sources for most assigned institutions, and several initiatives do not disclose exact budgets, user counts or implementation metrics. Overall, the evidence supports a medium-confidence R1 profile with enough public signals to compare the institution across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tools, workforce and equity dimensions.

UC Santa Barbara Google Gemini NotebookLM AI tools official
gaps_acknowledged: Public sources did not identify a standalone chief AI officer role during this sweep.

ACTION's NSF award and external compute allocation are public, but UCSB did not disclose a campus-owned AI GPU count, Gemini/NotebookLM active user counts, or enterprise contract value in the reviewed sources.
Leadership names were not populated because the assigned task prioritized missing AI posture profiles and every non-obvious AI claim is URL-cited.
